transcript:
## Deal Information

### Prospect
Sam Moore, partner at a multi-practice law firm in Northern Virginia (Alexandria HQ; new Fairfax office). ~11-12 total staff with 8 attorneys; three co-equal partners. Approaching ~$2M annual revenue in 2026 with steady YoY growth. Sam is shifting from practicing attorney to CEO/firm manager.

### Call Context
Sam engaged after reading SMB Team's Law Firm Growth Accelerator, receiving Five-Star Attorney, and attending the recent 2-day bootcamp. Seeking help to accelerate growth, especially in criminal defense lead generation, and to systematize the firm as he moves into a CEO role.

### Current State
- Multi-practice: Criminal Defense, Family Law, Civil/Business Litigation.
- Two offices: Alexandria (primary), Fairfax (opened end of last year; priority market).
- Early-stage Local Services Ads (LSA) for Criminal in-house; not fully verified; no agency support. No PPC currently. Mixed-history SEO; currently using a part-time overseas contractor.
- Intake: High conversion (estimated 80-90%) when they actually speak to prospects; speed-to-lead and follow-up inconsistent due to court/trial load and limited process.

### Pain Points
- Lead volume for Criminal Defense is insufficient, especially high-value DUIs and high-end misdemeanors in Fairfax.
- Speed-to-lead gaps: missed/slow callbacks when attorneys are in court or trials; follow-up process is weak, causing leakage.
- SEO/Maps underperformance: Google Business Profile primary category set to "Law Firm," not "Criminal Defense Lawyer"; weak GBP cover photo; missing key secondary categories; inconsistent NAP/hours across directories; thin/misaligned on-page SEO.
- Capacity planning: Need predictable, controllable lead flow they can throttle during jury trials.
- Scaling/operations: Need SOPs, metrics, and management cadence as Sam transitions to CEO.

### Specific Requirements
- Criminal-specific lead gen in Fairfax: LSA activation/verification and optimization; consider PPC alongside LSAs and Maps; negative keywords to avoid low-fee "cheap" leads; geographic precision for Fairfax.
- GBP optimization: Change primary category to Criminal Defense Lawyer; humanized headshot/partner photo as cover; add missing categories (Divorce Attorney, Mediation Service, Business Lawyer, etc.).
- Local SEO: Build city-level practice pages (Alexandria and Fairfax) with aligned Title/H1, >1,000 words, and internal linking.
- Review acceleration: Team training, scripting, bonuses ($25-$50 per review), and weekly cadence to grow GBP authority.
- Intake optimization: Improve responsiveness, tracking, and follow-up.

### Budget & Timeline
- Budget not provided. Sam indicated any investment must cover its expense near-term.
- Near-term: Criminal lead gen this quarter (next 90 days).
- Strategic: Avoid plateau in 2027; target >$2.8-$3.0M in 2027 after hitting ~$2.0M in 2026.

### Decision Maker
Three co-equal partners. Sam drives initiatives but requires partner approval. Sam to brief partners and reconvene for a decision discussion.

### Tech Stack
- Google Local Services Ads (in progress; in-house).
- Google Business Profile (two locations).
- Website with SEO content (contractor-supported).
- No stated intake platform/CRM or call answering solution.

### Case Values
- DUI ~$4,800; Reckless Driving ~$1,200; protective orders hourly.
- Capacity: 5-10 additional criminal cases/month feasible; can throttle during trial weeks.

### Competitive Landscape
- P&A Law dominates region with LSA+PPC; Price Benowitz/Karen Riley Porter use DBA/brand tactics; Patrick Anderson has strong Maps presence.

### Next Steps
- Dan Bryant to prepare tailored proposal options centered on Criminal lead gen for Fairfax (LSA verification/management, PPC, GBP/SEO quick wins, intake responsiveness) and share a concise audit recap and prioritized quick-win checklist Sam can share with partners.
- Sam Moore to review materials with partners and confirm availability for proposal review.
